hero.title: Math olympiads, reimagined
hero.subtitle: Compete, learn, and have fun with real problems from international competitions. For students aged 8 to 14.

step1.title: Sign up free
step1.description: In less than 2 minutes. No credit card, no strings attached.

step2.title: Solve problems
step2.description: Practice with hundreds of problems or join an official competition.

step3.title: Earn medals
step3.description: Download your certificate, climb the leaderboard, and show the world.

summerBowlLanding.title: Numoria Summer Bowl
summerBowlLanding.tagline: Enter the Bowl. Leave a champion.
summerBowlLanding.teacherWayBody: Create a class with your institutional account and bring your whole team into the bowl.
summerBowlLanding.studentWayBody: Sign up solo and compete on your own from home.
summerBowlLanding.prize: 🏆 Individual + school Perfect Score diplomas · Top results published on numoriachallenge.com
summerBowlLanding.emailSub: Leave us your email and we'll let you know as soon as registration opens.
summerBowlLanding.emailSpamFree: No spam. We only write when there's Summer Bowl news.
summerBowlLanding.successTitle: All set! We'll let you know when registration opens
summerBowlLanding.successBody: You'll get an email at {email} with all the Summer Bowl 2026 details.
summerBowlLanding.successDisclaimer: Creating an account takes 2 minutes. No credit card required.
summerBowlLanding.bowlsSubHeader: Three separate competitions — participate in one, two or all three. Each bowl has its own top 10.
summerBowlLanding.step1Body: In less than 2 minutes. No credit card, no commitments.
summerBowlLanding.step2Body: When registration opens you get the link and enter the bowl from home.
summerBowlLanding.step3Body: Individual Perfect Score diploma + school diploma if your school stands out. Top results published on numoriachallenge.com.
summerBowlLanding.b2bHeader: Are you a teacher or school principal?
summerBowlLanding.b2bBody: If you want to register your whole team or school in Summer Bowl 2026, write to us. We send invite codes, materials for your students and full support during the inaugural edition.
summerBowlLanding.faq1Q: Does it cost anything to participate?
summerBowlLanding.faq1A: No. The Inaugural Edition of Summer Bowl 2026 is 100% free. We don't ask for credit card or payment info at any point.
summerBowlLanding.faq2A: Students from Elementary (grades 4-6) and Middle School (grades 7-9). Each bowl has separate divisions per level.
summerBowlLanding.faq3A: Each student who achieves a Perfect Score (100% of the bowl) gets an individual diploma with 'Inaugural Edition' seal. Schools with the most Perfect Scores receive the 'School Perfect Score' diploma. Top results from each bowl are featured on numoriachallenge.com, and all participants get the permanent 'Founding Participant 2026' badge on their profile.
summerBowlLanding.faq4Q: Do I need to register as a team or can I participate solo?
summerBowlLanding.faq4A: You can participate individually or as part of a team organized by your teacher. If your school isn't registered yet, write to us at the teachers section.
summerBowlLanding.faq5A: Registration opens in May 2026. If you leave your email above, you'll be one of the first to know when we open it.
summerBowlLanding.faq6A: Starting November 2026, the 2026-2027 official cycle opens with 6 monthly contests on the road to the olympiads. Summer Bowl is the perfect warm-up so you arrive trained.
summerBowlLanding.finalCtaHeader: Ready to be part of the inaugural edition?
summerBowlLanding.finalCtaBody: Reserve your spot free. We'll let you know as soon as registration opens.
summerBowlLanding.finalCtaBodyShort: Start now. Account takes 2 minutes and is 100% free.
summerBowlLanding.finalCtaTipInstitutional: 💡 Tip: if you sign up with your institutional email (@yourschool.edu), we auto-assign the teacher role.

summerBowl.heroBody: Three free competitions this summer (June–July) before the 2026-2027 official cycle opens. Compete in any of the three bowls and earn a diploma with the "Inaugural Edition" seal plus the permanent Founding Participant 2026 badge on your profile.
summerBowl.heroPrize: 🏆 Individual + school Perfect Score diplomas · Top results published on numoriachallenge.com

practicesPage.title: 📚 Free Practice
practicesPage.description: Always-available training tests. They don't count toward the official ranking or XP — they're for your students to build confidence before the contests.
practicesPage.empty: No practices available at the moment.
practicesPage.primaryDescription: For students in grades 4-6. No calculator.
practicesPage.middleDescription: For students in grades 7-9. Two variants depending on calculator use.

officialsPage.title: 🏆 Official Contests 2026-2027
officialsPage.description: Six official competitions during the school year. Results are published and updated monthly, and count toward the national and regional ranking.
officialsPage.emptyDescription: No official contests have been generated in the system yet. The 6 contests of the school year will be published before the first event.

paperEntryPage.title: 📝 Manual entry
paperEntryPage.description: Transcribe to the platform the answers your students wrote on paper for the official contests. Select the contest and then enter the answers student by student. Practices don't need transcription — students complete them directly in the app.
paperEntryPage.emptyMessage: No active official contests at the moment. The first contest of the 2026-2027 school year is Saturday November 7, 2026.

about.title: About Numoria
about.intro: Numoria Challenge is an online math olympiad platform for students aged 8 to 14. We turn math practice into an adventure: competitions, medals, and a guide character named Numa who accompanies every student.
about.mission: We believe every child can enjoy math when they learn by playing. We want schools across Latin America to have access to quality math competitions, no matter their budget or location.
forWho: Elementary and middle school students (ages 8 to 14) who want to challenge themselves and improve.
Teachers looking to motivate their groups with ready-to-use competitions.
Schools that want to offer math olympiads without setting up all the logistics.

help.title: Help center
help.intro: We answer the most common questions. If you can't find what you're looking for, write to us and we'll help.

faqs.q: How do I create an account?
faqs.a: Click "Create account" from the home page, choose whether you're a student or a teacher, and follow the steps. It's free to get started.

faqs.q: How does a student join a team?
faqs.a: The teacher creates the team and shares an 8-character invite code. The student enters that code from their account to join.
You can start for free with several trial competitions, no credit card required. Later we'll offer plans for schools with more teams and features.

faqs.q: How many teams and students can I have?
faqs.a: By default each school can create up to 2 teams, with a maximum of 15 students per team (30 students total).

faqs.q: Do I need to install anything?
faqs.a: No. Numoria works from your browser on computers, tablets, and phones. All you need is an internet connection.

help.contactNote: Question not listed here? Write to us at mimathonline@gmail.com and we'll reply as soon as possible.

privacy.title: Privacy policy
privacy.intro: This is a brief version while we prepare the full one. At Numoria Challenge we protect the data of students, teachers, and schools. We only collect what's needed for the platform to work.

points.t: Name or nickname, email, role (student or teacher), school, and competition results. We don't ask for unnecessary sensitive data.
To create your account, organize teams and competitions, show your results, and improve the platform. We don't sell your data to third parties.
Numoria is designed for children. We recommend that teachers and families accompany the sign-up. Students can use a nickname instead of their real name.
You can request access, correction, or deletion of your data at any time by writing to mimathonline@gmail.com.
We store information on secure servers and limit access only to those who need it to operate the service.

privacy.note: By using Numoria Challenge you accept this policy. If you have questions, write to us at mimathonline@gmail.com.

terms.title: Terms of service
terms.intro: This is a brief version of our terms while we prepare the full one. By using Numoria Challenge you accept these conditions.

points.t: Numoria Challenge is for educational purposes. You agree to use it respectfully and not to cheat in competitions.
You are responsible for keeping your account confidential. Teachers are responsible for the teams they create and the codes they share.
The problems, materials, and the Numoria brand are ours. Your results belong to you, and we show them inside the platform for your tracking.
During the pilot stage the service may have interruptions or changes. We'll do our best to give advance notice of any important change.
We may update these terms. We'll post the date of the last update and, if the change is important, we'll let you know.

terms.note: Questions about these terms? Write to us at mimathonline@gmail.com.

contact.title: Contact
contact.intro: Have questions, ideas, or want to bring Numoria to your school? We'd love to hear from you.
contact.responseTime: We usually reply within 1 to 2 business days.
